programme.title: Bushwick
programme.desc: Lucy emerges from a Brooklyn subway to find that her neighborhood is under attack by black-clad military soldiers. An ex-Marine corpsman, Stupe, reluctantly helps her fight for survival through a civil war as Texas attempts to secede from the U.S.

programme.title: Kindred
programme.desc: Plagued by mysterious hallucinations, a pregnant woman suspects that the family of her deceased boyfriend has intentions for her unborn child.

programme.title: Spare Parts
programme.desc: Weapons attached to one arm, four young women must fight like gladiators as a bloodthirsty crowd cheers them on.

programme.title: Radioflash
programme.desc: In a dystopian future, a tech-savvy teenager and her father flee the city and seek refuge with her grandfather deep in the mountains of the Pacific Northwest.

programme.title: Revenge
programme.desc: A shocking act leaves a woman brutalized and left for dead by three sleazy men. Unfortunately for her assailants, she survives and soon begins a relentless quest for bloody revenge.

programme.title: The Postcard Killings
programme.desc: A New York detective investigates the death of his daughter, who was murdered while on her honeymoon in London, the victim of an apparent serial killer.

programme.title: Extraterrestrial
programme.desc: Malevolent aliens interrupt five collegians' vacation at a remote woodland cabin.

programme.title: The Shrine
programme.desc: The search for a missing teen in Poland leads a rookie reporter (Cindy Sampson) and her companions to an encounter with deadly cultists.

programme.title: Area 407
programme.desc: The survivors of an airplane crash are trapped in a government testing area filled with predators.

programme.title: The Other Lamb
programme.desc: A young woman born into an all-female cult begins to question the teachings of her leader.

programme.title: Welcome to Willits
programme.desc: Locals in the California woods are attacked by supernatural creatures, and when the situation escalates into carnage, a farmer struggles to defend his family.

programme.title: Revenge
programme.desc: A shocking act leaves a woman brutalized and left for dead by three sleazy men. Unfortunately for her assailants, she survives and soon begins a relentless quest for bloody revenge.

programme.title: Ride
programme.desc: Struggling actor James pays his bills by driving for a ride-sharing service in Los Angeles. His night soon takes a shocking turn when Bruno, armed with a gun, takes James and a woman on a terrifying, white-knuckle trip that spirals out of control.

programme.title: VFW
programme.desc: A typical night for veterans at a VFW turns into a battle for survival when a desperate teen runs into the bar. When a gang of punks come looking for her, the vets use every weapon at their disposal to protect themselves from an unrelenting attack.

programme.title: Beneath the Darkness
programme.desc: Teenagers discover a terrible secret when they break into the home of a mortician whose wife died two years earlier.

programme.title: Once Upon a Time in Venice
programme.desc: To get his dog back, private eye Steve Ford forges an unlikely alliance with Spyder, the leader of a local gang. With help from his best friend, Steve pulls out the big guns to retrieve Spyder's missing cash and cocaine and save his beloved pooch.

programme.title: White Elephant
programme.desc: When two cops witness an assassination attempt, a ruthless crime boss orders Gabriel Tancredi, an ex-Marine turned mob enforcer, to eliminate any and all threats.

programme.title: Baskin
programme.desc: Policemen (Gorkem Kasal, Ergun Kuyucu) face a night of horror in a subterranean labyrinth after answering a distress call.

programme.title: No Man's Land
programme.desc: When a vigilante border patrol turns fatal, a man flees on horseback to Mexico, seeking forgiveness from the victim's father.

programme.title: A Score to Settle
programme.desc: Diagnosed with a fatal condition, a mob enforcer leaves prison after 19 years and plots a bloody course of revenge against his old gang.

programme.title: Brawl in Cell Block 99
programme.desc: A desperate former boxer takes work as a drug courier and winds up in a jail where his enemies force him to commit acts of violence.

programme.title: Desolation
programme.desc: When a widow ventures into a remote wilderness area with her son and best friend to spread her husband's ashes, a mysterious hiker begins dogging them and forces them to confront their deepest fears.

programme.title: Grabbers
programme.desc: Residents of an island off the coast of Ireland learn that the only way to survive an invasion of blood-sucking aliens is to stay drunk.

programme.title: Muzzle
programme.desc: A Los Angeles police officer and his K-9 partner try to uncover a vast conspiracy that has a chokehold on the city.

programme.title: Section 8

programme.desc: A shocking act leaves a woman brutalized and left for dead by three sleazy men. Unfortunately for her assailants, she survives and soon begins a relentless quest for bloody revenge.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Narrowboater Robbie Cumming begins his 170-mile journey across the north of England and soon runs into difficulty when he encounters a problem with his craft's propeller in Wigan and runs aground in Blackburn. Nevertheless, he pushes on to navigate a true wonder of the waterways, the Burnley Embankment.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo joins Navy Wings pilots for a spectacular close formation flight in the skies over Royal Naval Air Station Yeovilton. He also visits the magnificent Elizabethan mansion of Longleat, seat of the Marquesses of Bath, to find out about the estate’s remarkable survival after the Second World War. At Chippenham in Wiltshire, Michael pays a call on a signalling manufacturer that has been at the heart of British railway projects for decades and has a go on the centre’s simulator, driving the Elizabeth Line train to Paddington.

programme.title: Universe
programme.desc: It’s the ultimate question: why are we here? Brian Cox explores how cutting-edge space missions take us back 13.8 billion years to the origin of the universe.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches England’s south coast to continue his exploration of postwar Britain. He begins in Dorset on the Swanage Railway, riding a glorious 1940s steam locomotive on the trail of one of Britain’s most popular children’s authors, Enid Blyton. In Poole, Michael discovers an icon of the Swinging Sixties – the lava lamp, invented in the town by an eccentric accountant. At Romsey, Michael heads to the magnificent Broadlands Estate, once home of Louis Mountbatten, first Earl Mountbatten of Burma. Michael’s last stop on this leg of the journey is Portchester, on the northern edge of Portsmouth Harbour, where he visits a factory manufacturing a marine craft pioneered in the 1950s – the hovercraft.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Narrowboater and film-maker Robbie Cumming crosses the Pennines on the picturesque Leeds and Liverpool Canal, and lends a hand to hire boaters in Gargrave, North Yorkshire. He also encounters Bingley Five Rise Locks, a wonder of the inland waterways.

programme.title: Gods and Monsters: Homer's Odyssey
programme.desc: Virginia Woolf said that Homer's epic poem the Odyssey was 'alive to every tremor and gleam of existence'. Following the magical and strange adventures of warrior king Odysseus, inventor of the idea of the Trojan horse, the poem can claim to be the greatest story ever told. Now British poet Simon Armitage goes on his own Greek adventure, following in the footsteps of one of his own personal heroes. Yet Simon ponders the question of whether he even likes the guy.

programme.title: Bletchley Park: Codebreaking's Forgotten Genius
programme.desc: Documentary looking at Gordon Welchman, a codebreaker crucial to the allies defeating the Nazis in World War II. Filmed extensively at Bletchley Park.

programme.title: Blade Runner
programme.desc: Ridley Scott's sci-fi classic. Los Angeles, 2019. Mankind has succeeded in creating replicants - near-perfect humanoids superior in intelligence even to humans - but as a result of a mutiny on a human colony in outer space, it is now illegal for unlicensed replicants to be on Earth. The punishment, if they are caught, is death. Four replicants have escaped and fled back to Earth, and android hunter or 'blade runner' Deckard is charged with identifying and 'retiring' them...

programme.title: Jane Eyre
programme.desc: Charlotte Bronte's classic about an orphan girl who grows up to become a governess in a gloomy manor in Yorkshire, where she falls in love with the mysterious Edward Rochester.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Narrowboater and film-maker Robbie Cumming crosses the Pennines on the picturesque Leeds and Liverpool Canal, and lends a hand to hire boaters in Gargrave, North Yorkshire. He also encounters Bingley Five Rise Locks, a wonder of the inland waterways.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches England’s south coast to continue his exploration of postwar Britain. He begins in Dorset on the Swanage Railway, riding a glorious 1940s steam locomotive on the trail of one of Britain’s most popular children’s authors, Enid Blyton. In Poole, Michael discovers an icon of the Swinging Sixties – the lava lamp, invented in the town by an eccentric accountant. At Romsey, Michael heads to the magnificent Broadlands Estate, once home of Louis Mountbatten, first Earl Mountbatten of Burma. Michael’s last stop on this leg of the journey is Portchester, on the northern edge of Portsmouth Harbour, where he visits a factory manufacturing a marine craft pioneered in the 1950s – the hovercraft.

programme.title: Top of the Pops: Big Hits 1989
programme.desc: Performances from the Top of the Pops vaults featuring the movers and shakers of 1989. [S]

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Simon Bates and Richard Skinner present the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 4 July 1985. Featuring Tears for Fears, Simply Red, Dead or Alive, Fine Young Cannibals and The Damned.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Bruno Brookes presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 11 July 1991 and featuring OMD, Billy Bragg, Bros and Bryan Adams.

programme.title: Words and Music
programme.desc: Tracey MacLeod introduces a series celebrating the art of songwriting in which classic pop songs are stripped back to basics and performed by their original composers. Iggy Pop - I Wanna Be Your Dog Richard Berry - Louie Louie Bee Gees - To Love Somebody Tony Joe Wright - Rainy Night in Georgia Sparks - This Town Ain't Big Enough for Both of Us Jimmy Webb - By the Time I Get to Phoenix Neil Finn - Don't Dream It's Over Debbie Harry and Chris Stein - Heart of Glass

programme.title: The Old Grey Whistle Test
programme.desc: Blondie perform live at the height of their powers in 1979 at the Apollo Theatre in Glasgow, with Debbie Harry wowing the audience with such hits as Dreaming, Union City Blue, Atomic, Picture This, Heart of Glass, Hanging on the Telephone and Sunday Girl.

programme.title: In Concert
programme.desc: American songwriter and performer Jimmy Webb sings some of his hit compositions, including All My Love's Laughter, By the Time I Get to Phoenix, Wichita Lineman and MacArthur Park.

programme.title: Glastonbury
programme.desc: BBC Music brings you The Glastonbury Experience 2021. A musical extravaganza celebrating the spirit, the performances and the magic of Worthy Farm and Glastonbury Festival

programme.title: Women's Test Cricket
programme.desc: Highlights of day two of the Lord's Test match between England Women and India Women.

programme.title: Horizon: Oceans of the Solar...
programme.desc: The oceans define the earth. They are crucial to life and we used to think that they were unique to our blue planet. But we were wrong. It has recently been discovered that there are oceans all over our solar system, and they are very similar to our own. And now scientists are going on an epic journey in search of new life in places that never seemed possible. Nasa is even planning to dive to the depths of a strange, distant ocean in a remarkable submarine. Horizon discovers that the hunt for oceans in space is marking the dawn of a new era in the search for alien life.

programme.title: Life on Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the role of a few of the millions of insect species, some of which have developed extremely close relationships with plants. Insects pollinate flowers and in some cases neither flower nor insect can survive without the other.

programme.title: The Sentinels
programme.desc: In 1915, soldier Gabriel Ferraud is badly injured on the front lines. He is then selected to join a top-secret army research project: the Sentinels program. With this research comes Dyxenal, a serum that creates a whole new breed of soldier. In French and German with English subtitles. 1915, France - after sustaining severe injuries on the WWI battle field, soldier Gabriel Ferraud is unwittingly selected to join a top-secret army research project aimed at creating a whole new breed of soldier: the Sentinels. In French with English subtitles.

programme.title: The Sentinels
programme.desc: Gabriel must join the Sentinels brigade if he ever wants to be reunited with his family, who believe him dead. An attack on the secret military laboratory prompts an investigation, much to the displeasure of Colonel Mirreau, who heads the Sentinels program. In French and German with English subtitles.

programme.title: The Black Forest Murders
programme.desc: When DCI Barbara Kramer and her colleague Thomas Riedle get the message that a young woman from Buchingen has not returned home from a run, the local force and concerned villagers have already been searching for her for 17 hours. They face an uphill struggle to prevent evidence from being destroyed, a contingency which becomes more salient when a body is discovered 72 hours later. In German with English subtitles.

programme.title: The Black Forest Murders
programme.desc: Despite the team working many hours of overtime on the Berghoff case, there are still few leads to follow. The forensic team is able to isolate the DNA of an unknown male, but the databases do not provide a match. The discovery of a second body less than 20 miles away leads the team to believe a serial killer is at work. In German with English subtitles

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Sitcom about Hyacinth Bucket, an obsessive snob, and her embarrassing family. Hyacinth makes some very expensive holiday plans before dashing off to fulfil her voluntary church cleaning duties with the Ladies' Guild.

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Unexpected visitors drop in and mistakenly suspect Victor of killing an elderly blind man. A concert gives Victor the chance to use his ventriloquist's dummy.

programme.title: AI Decoded
programme.desc: The race between America and China to lead the world in artificial intelligence is reshaping the global balance of power. But behind the headlines, a hidden conflict is unfolding. Join AI Decoded as we examine the seizure of $13 million worth of advanced AI chips allegedly bound for China, and Anthropic's claim that Alibaba carried out the largest known AI 'distillation attack' in history. As nations and tech giants compete for supremacy, the battle for AI is increasingly being fought in the shadows. Who will win the race for AI dominance, and how far will they go to get there?

programme.title: Tech Now
programme.desc: Adrienne Murray has rare access to Microsoft's Quantum Lab in Denmark, Ammie Sekhon checks out the action and innovation at this year's Red Bull Soapbox Race, Melissa Hogenboom visits the UK's first Longevity Show to explore the boom in personalised nutrients, and Andrew Rogers visits the UK aerospace company developing a nuclear fusion-powered rocket.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: China's Greatest Treasures
programme.desc: Dragon is the ultimate symbol of China. Chinese people often call themselves the descendants of the dragon. Unlike in western mythology where dragons are depicted as monsters to be tamed, in China, dragons are seen as life-giving, beneficent and auspicious. Once you know about them, you notice them everywhere in China. In this episode, Alastair explores the art of dragons, to understand how this mythical creature has become so ingrained in Chinese national identity.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: What Happened at...?
programme.desc: On 1 July 1916, nearly 20,000 British soldiers were killed on the first day of the Battle of the Somme – one of the bloodiest days in the history of warfare and a lasting symbol of both sacrifice and futility. In this documentary, Jordan Dunbar examines how communities have been shaped by the battle and reveals how its legacy continues to resonate through families - including his own.

programme.title: The Media Show
programme.desc: A global, topical weekly show that lifts the hood on the entertainment and news business. The Media Show explores the companies and personalities who command our attention, and how our perception of the world is shaped by their actions. From the streaming giants who entertain us, to the news outlets and social media platforms that inform and enrage us, The Media Show explains how the industry really works.

programme.title: BBC News

programme.title: The Travel Show
programme.desc: The Zoological Society of London - the charity that runs London Zoo - is celebrating its 200th birthday this year. So, in an age when people are much more attuned to the welfare of animals, Roma Wells asks: what are zoos for? She sees for herself some of the pioneering innovation ZSL achieved in years gone by, some of the work it does today to rescue threatened species, and finds out what's next for animal attractions everywhere.

programme.desc: As football fever sweeps the globe, Paul Carter discovers how technology is transforming the beautiful game and leaving a legacy for future generations. Kicking off at Boston Dynamics, Paul meets the robots redefining matchday and goes up against 'robot Ronaldo'. In Philadelphia, he visits the university students using AI to unlock new levels of sporting performance. And at TOCA in California, he puts cutting-edge ball technology to the test, alongside the stars of tomorrow. From artists to innovators, join Paul as he meets the pioneering people shaping the future of football.

programme.title: Elvis
programme.desc: An epic, big-screen spectacle from Baz Luhrmann that explores the life and music of Elvis Presley. In a thoroughly cinematic drama, Elvis's story is seen through the lens of his complicated relationship with his enigmatic manager, Colonel Tom Parker. As told by Parker, the film delves into the tangled dynamic between the two, spanning over 20 years, from Presley's rise to fame to his unprecedented stardom, against the backdrop of the evolving cultural landscape.

programme.title: Expert Witness
programme.desc: An expert witness uses fibre analysis to solve an execution-style murder, and after 26 years in prison, a man walks free when a forensic scientist links DNA to the real killer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: We return to Norfolk to see if two brothers can finish transforming a pair of old farm cottages into one luxury home. Dion meets a developer in Northampton who has grand plans of his own, and in Kingston upon Thames, Jacqui likes the plans a new owner has for a one-bedroom flat.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Viewers will be checking their change after watching this episode in Kent! David Harper meets a coin collector who reveals which rare 50 pence piece is worth 300 times its face value! John Wilson’s collection boasts 11,000 coins, including early medieval and Tudor hammered coins and the very first milled ones from the 18th century. Hoping to splash some cash of their own at Detling antiques fair are two teams of bargain hunters. In red are mum and daughter Nisha and dancer Adzi, with their expert Mark Stacey. Their opponents in blue are partners and musicians Kathy and Nardo. Nardo used to play drums in 1980s band Amazulu, and Kathy worked with some big recording artists of the era. They hope to compose a win with expert Kate Bliss, kicking off with a dolphin water feature, but four figures is too much to splash out on. The reds, meanwhile, are pinning their hopes on a set of cameo brooches, but Mark has doubts whether they will prove popular at auction. In Windsor, David hears auctioneer Harry Ballin’s estimates before the teams see their items go under the hammer – for a profit or a loss!

programme.title: Anna Haugh’s Big Irish Food Tour
programme.desc: A mainstay of television and radio, Vanessa Feltz takes every opportunity to escape London for her Irish oasis and is keen for Anna to experience the culinary delights of east Cork. Vanessa sends Anna to the Comeragh Mountains as it's lambing season and the farmer needs help bringing sheep off the mountain where they have been grazing. Vanessa takes Anna to Ballycotton Harbour to sample the catch of the day. In the kitchen, Anna has the perfect dish in mind for her guide to enjoy.

The celebrities competing for the Pointless trophy are Larry Dean, Holly Walsh, Desiree Burch, Jessica Fostekew, Morgan Lake, Ashleigh Nelson, Beatie Edney and Michelle Collins.

programme.title: The Weakest Link
programme.desc: Hosted by Romesh Ranganathan, eight celebrities’ general knowledge is put to the test in order to bank as much money for charity. programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: The action shifts to Steve's home turf as the Deadly 60 team scour the UK in search of its lethal beauties. From the wild seas off the Pembrokeshire coast to the New Forest, Steve proves that British wildlife is anything but boring. Gannets prove to be more than a match for Steve in a diving competition. He battles with the elements to reach the birds but the rough seas are determined to stop him. He then has to confront his past as he comes face to face with the only venomous snake that has hospitalised him. The Deadly team also have a close call as they uncover a hornets' nest in a loft, and Steve gets a taste of what it is like to be prey for a day as he is hunted through a forest by a hungry goshawk.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Max scrambles to excuse his behaviour. Priya receives a shock. Kim and Howie confront Sheila.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Motoring news and views from the usual team. Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May travel to the US to take three new generation muscle cars on an epic road trip from San Francisco to the heart of Utah. And after the excitement of their last American road trip, this time they do their best to avoid letting a mob of angry locals pelt them with rocks. Meanwhile, back in the UK Jeremy tests the feisty new Fiat 500 Abarth and pop idol Will Young is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: The Traitors US
programme.desc: While a new alliance is tested, suspicions force an old one into the spotlight. The mission puts the Traitors under pressure, and one player receives some unwanted attention. The creepiest murder yet is about to be sprung upon one of the Faithful.

programme.title: The Traitors US
programme.desc: With a place in the final at stake, emotions are running high as friendships are tested to their limits. New suspicions run through the castle. The mission gets the players' heads in a spin, and one Faithful goes down in flames.

programme.title: Louis Theroux
programme.desc: Louis Theroux is in America to interview members of the Church of Scientology - but they do not want to speak with him. So he meets disaffected former members of the controversial organisation and uncomfortably recreates some extreme experiences. All the while, Louis is being oddly shadowed by people who might be the very folk he wouldn't mind having a word with...

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Motoring news and views from the usual team. Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May travel to the US to take three new generation muscle cars on an epic road trip from San Francisco to the heart of Utah. And after the excitement of their last American road trip, this time they do their best to avoid letting a mob of angry locals pelt them with rocks. Meanwhile, back in the UK Jeremy tests the feisty new Fiat 500 Abarth and pop idol Will Young is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: Things You Should Have Done
programme.desc: Stay-at-home-daughter Chi is suddenly forced to fend for herself when her parents die in a car crash. Chi learns she will only inherit the house on condition she completes a list of 'Things You Should Have Done' left in the parents' will, the first of which is, ironically, learn to drive. Meanwhile, Chi's highly strung auntie Karen wants nothing to do with her dependent pest of a niece, but very much wants the house she feels is rightfully hers. Chi ventures out into Ramsgate in search of a driving license and infiltrates the family of a mild-mannered driving instructor in the process.

programme.title: Things You Should Have Done
programme.desc: Chi spectacularly fails a trial period at the pharmacy with Karen, but Dave kindly lets her shadow him on his delivery rounds. On a drop-off to a retirement home, Chi finds her tribe... old people. She also falls in love with Linda, an elderly lady who seems to think Chi is her husband. Elsewhere, Dave has a health scare, and Karen starts to think she might be the fertility issue.

programme.title: Things You Should Have Done
programme.desc: Pancake day is a highly sentimental time for Chi. Dave confides in Lucas about his health scare. They vow to make the most of every moment, and that begins with pancake day.

programme.title: Things You Should Have Done
programme.desc: Shock horror when Chi discovers bills are a thing, and her electricity and water get cancelled. As a result, she decides she's just going to live at auntie Karen's.

programme.title: Things You Should Have Done
programme.desc: Chi organises a family camping holiday to help Karen relax, since she was 'diagnosed uptight' and told she’s unlikely to get pregnant if she doesn’t lower her blood pressure.

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: The action shifts to Steve's home turf as the Deadly 60 team scour the UK in search of its lethal beauties. Terry Nutkins lends a helping hand in spotting otters and tells Steve the story of how he lost two fingers to one of these creatures. A bit of pond-dipping reveals a whole host of the nasties ready to bite, maim and generally eat each other, and Steve gets a chance to meet with the biggest spider in the UK. Then it's off for a high-speed chase as Steve plays pigeon for the afternoon and nearly loses his head whilst being hunted by the fastest animal on the planet, the truly incredible peregrine falcon.

programme.title: Marcel the Shell with Shoes On
programme.desc: When struggling film-maker Dean moves into a rental property, he quickly discovers he is not alone. Marcel, an adorable one-inch-tall shell, already lives there with his grandmother Connie and their pet lint Alan. Once part of a sprawling community of shells, Marcel and Connie live alone as the sole survivors of a mysterious tragedy. Taken with his unlikely new friend, Dean starts filming Marcel and his grandmother, igniting a meteoric rise to internet fame that changes their lives forever. But will Marcel’s newfound sense of possibility and a throng of superfans be enough to track down his long-lost family?

programme.title: Ludwig
programme.desc: With new information about Chief Constable Ziegler, John is sent back to the police station to get closer to DI Russell Carter, his brother’s new partner who mysteriously arrived around the same time that James disappeared. A new case takes John and Russell on a walking tour of Cambridge, where they are faced with a conundrum: why would someone kill a tour guide that they’d only just met? To get closer to the truth about what happened to conspiracy blogger Roger Sinclair, Lucy goes undercover and makes a shocking discovery of her own.

programme.title: Amandaland
programme.desc: Amanda meets Mal’s ex Abs for the first time and then discovers that Abs found her shallow. So she decides to use a school careers talk to prove her wrong.

programme.title: Amandaland
programme.desc: Amanda is thrilled when a hipster coffee shop opens in Soha and offers to promote the cafe on Senous. Meanwhile, Anne unexpectedly finds fame after a video she’s posted goes viral.

programme.title: Amandaland
programme.desc: Amanda finds a used condom in a sofa she’s disposing of and tries to work out which family member family could have left it there.

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous: The Movie
programme.desc: Worried about her finances when her book fails to find a publisher, Patsy is given the tip that Kate Moss has left her PR company and will be looking for new representation. Things start to go awry when the plot to approach Kate goes horribly wrong, and Patsy and Edina find themselves vilified by the entire nation and having to flee the country. Madcap comedy from BBC Films based on the TV series.

programme.title: Absolutely Fabulous: The Movie
programme.desc: Worried about her finances when her book fails to find a publisher, Patsy is given the tip that Kate Moss has left her PR company and will be looking for new representation. Things start to go awry when the plot to approach Kate goes horribly wrong, and Patsy and Edina find themselves vilified by the entire nation and having to flee the country. Madcap comedy from BBC Films based on the TV series.

programme.title: Back to Life
programme.desc: When Miri Matteson returns home after eighteen years, can she integrate back into her old life? With a terrible event from her past hanging over her, it won’t be easy.

programme.title: Back to Life
programme.desc: After the awkward end to their not-a-date date, Miri offers to help Billy sort through Anna’s things. Although picking through the personal belongings of your crush’s dead wife might seem awful to most, Miri takes it in her stride - until she makes a very personal discovery. Things get worse for Miri when Janice tells her that she needs a bank account before she can start working at the supermarket. Miri does have a bank account but hasn’t used it since 2001. Unfortunately, the branded piggy banks she was given all those years ago are no longer relevant, and once again Miri must navigate a world she barely recognises.

programme.title: Back to Life
programme.desc: The morning after she was knocked off her bike by a mysterious car, Miri is convinced that John Boback was the one who drove into her, but her parents are not sure. When Oscar lets slip that the Bobacks are organising a vigil for Lara, Miri takes this as more proof that John is trying to ensure the town never forgets what Miri has done. Caroline is also preoccupied with Lara’s vigil, and with Oscar focused on Make Hythe Clean, she decides to return to the Ladies of Hythe. This time she brings biscuits. In a bid to put the vigil out of her mind, Miri hitches a ride along with Billy for the day, but is shocked when they arrive at his mother’s birthday party. After a failed attempt at hiding, Miri has no choice but to meet Gaia, who miraculously seems to be a fan. At the party, Miri bumps into someone from her past who reminds her that she doesn’t need to pretend to be something she’s not.

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005–2022)
programme.desc: Captured by Vikings, the Doctor and Clara must help protect their village from space warriors from the future, the Mire. Outnumbered and outgunned, their fate seems inevitable. So why is the Doctor preoccupied with a single Viking girl?

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005–2022)
programme.desc: England, 1651. Deadly highwayman 'the Knightmare' and his sidekick stalk the dark streets of London. But when they find loot that is not of this world, they come face to face with the Doctor. Who is the Knightmare in league with? And can the Doctor avoid the hangman's noose and protect the Earth from a devilish betrayal?

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee makes no effort to talk to other parents in the playground, and Lucy is worried that he is becoming isolated and friendless. She fires him up to go into school and make some new friends. Reluctantly Lee gives it a try. He opens up with strangers and makes friends with another lone male, Keith. But Keith is even less sociable than Lee.

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Confined to a wheelchair, his right leg encased in plaster after knee surgery, Lee has too much time on his hands, which he uses to watch the world go by through a pair of high-powered binoculars. He soon realises that all is not well in his neighbour’s house opposite. In fact, he is quickly convinced that something despicable is going on.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Moira’s younger sister Deedee makes a surprise visit, and the two pick up where they left off: jealous and resentful of each other. David is pressed to step out of his comfort zone by trying to help one of Jocelyn’s students who is having a hard time fitting in. Meanwhile, Alexis meddles in Mutt’s relationship with Twyla from the cafe by giving her some unsolicited advice. When Twyla takes it to heart, she decides to break up with Mutt, leaving Alexis to suffer the fallout.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: It’s Moira’s birthday, and Johnny plans on getting the entire family to help throw a surprise party. But the cover story of a charity fundraiser draws Moira to get involved in the planning. David must keep his fussy mother from taking complete control and insulting the locals who volunteered to put the event together. Alexis and Johnny have some father-daughter time when they visit Elmdale to pick up the birthday cake.

programme.title: Schitt's Creek
programme.desc: Johnny has finally found a way out of Schitt’s Creek, and his name is Andy, a businessman who plans on buying the town. All that Johnny needs now is Andy’s signature, but Roland hits it off with the guy, delaying the signing. Meanwhile, Alexis, knowing she is escaping, finds things complicated by Ted proposing and her finally revealing her true feelings to Mutt. David, feeling abandoned by his family and best friend Stevie, leaves town in Roland's pick-up.

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: All over Dublin, mothers are excitedly looking forward to seeing their loved ones return home for Christmas. But Agnes Brown is depressed. Her son Trevor is on the missions in Canada and can't possibly afford to make it back. With Maria's posh mum Hillary pressing the newlyweds to spend Christmas at her place, Agnes decides to compete for their love by providing an even more lavish Christmas celebration than Hillary's - even if Buster is having trouble delivering the turkey.

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: After faking Grandad's death, the Brown family are still waiting for the insurance money to come through. Agnes has promised the money to Dermot and Maria, who are desperate for a place of their own, but the insurance company is starting to get suspicious. Will Agnes be able to keep Grandad out of sight, or will one of the family give the game away?

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: After his dream Norway job doesn’t go quite to plan, Richard just wants to hide away from the world. Sadly for him, Ken has other ideas - mainly forcing him to join the protest group fighting to save the local allotments.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: A depressed Richard searches for a real job and embarks on a fitness journey as his 40th birthday looms. Meanwhile, Ken prepares for his first first date in almost 60 years.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Ken needs to resit his driving exam in order to win back his licence. Richard offers to take him out on a mock test, not realising just how rusty his dad has become behind the wheel.

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay keeps the questions coming as the week hits its midpoint. TV presenter Owain Wyn Evans, Olympic gold medallist Greg Rutherford and Blue Peter’s Abby Cook are back for more as the quiz pulls into Paisley, shining a light on the town’s history and hidden gems. Paul Gorton resumes his role as uncertified tour guide, blending unbelievable facts with playfully planted untruths as the overall leaderboard continues to take shape.

programme.title: What Happened at...?
programme.desc: On 1 July 1916, nearly 20,000 British soldiers were killed on the first day of the Battle of the Somme – one of the bloodiest days in the history of warfare and a lasting symbol of both sacrifice and futility. In this documentary, Jordan Dunbar examines how communities have been shaped by the battle and reveals how its legacy continues to resonate through families - including his own.

programme.title: Inside Barlinnie
programme.desc: After 18 months behind bars, Sean returns to his community in Cambuslang, where he struggles to manage his ADHD, battles with addiction and faces a court case for armed robbery. Self-styled vigilante Frankie lives by his own brutal code of 'jail justice', which sees him attacking sex offenders, while violent offender and drug user Owen attempts to rebuild his life on the outside, until his violent past catches up with him.

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Joe Swash goes in search of his Italian roots. Along the way, he finds stories of murder, banditry, an inspiring political activist and a family bar in southern Italy.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Expert Witness
programme.desc: A voice analyst makes a positive link to the killer of two police officers, and a lead forensic scientist identifies a phone snatcher from a shoe he left behind.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Programme in which experts uncover the various tricks of the property auction trade

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As Adam Frost’s brand new garden starts to take shape, it’s time to get some shrubs in the ground and construct a path. He also makes a start on planting up a potted herb garden and shares some ideas on what to grow against a wall, whether it be sunny or shady. At home in Devon, Carol Klein is mixing it up with flowers and edibles, where sweet peas and cosmos happily mingle with beetroot, basil and beans. And at West Dean Gardens in Chichester, Jamie Butterworth reveals the secrets of growing that border belle, the delphinium. In Birmingham, a lover of roses has created a garden full to brimming with beautiful blooms, while a gardener in Norwich shares her contemporary cottage garden that became a refuge when she needed it most.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The big political issues of the day.

programme.title: Wimbledon
programme.desc: Live coverage of the Championships at the All England Lawn Tennis Club at Wimbledon.

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Rivers
programme.desc: On this Grand Tour, Paul explores the lower reaches of Royal Deeside. Heading downstream, he runs aground in a canoe and then gets stuck trying to thread his body through an ancient fertility stone. In the village of Banchory, he breathes in the scent of lavender, and then takes a train along the Royal Deeside Line before heading into Aberdeen where he learns a secret recipe for the city’s famous rowie - a Caledonian version of the croissant. Journey’s end is on the city’s famous golden sands.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Max scrambles to excuse his behaviour. Priya receives a shock. Kim and Howie confront Sheila.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: At home in the Yorkshire Dales, Emmerdale stars and best mates Matthew Wolfenden and Roxy Shahidi take on the Antiques Road Trip challenge. Rolling in a retro Riley sports saloon, they're romping around the rolling hills in search of antique bargains. Paired up with super-fan Hettie Jago and legend of the game Steven Moore, they embark on a battle of the paperweights. And the competition steps up a gear when our feisty foursome find themselves around an original Monopoly board. Who's going to get the boot?

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: A ten-year-old boy is swept out to sea on a paddleboard in North Berwick. His parents have rushed out to try and save him, but all three of them are now struggling in the water. In Walmer, a yacht has run aground on the notorious Goodwin Sands. It’s a race against time to get the three casualties onto the safety of the lifeboat. On the Sussex coast, an urgent call has come in for two men trapped by the incoming tide beneath cliffs four miles east of Newhaven. And on the banks of the River Thames in London, the Teddington crew get more than they bargained for when they come to the aid of an injured cyclist - who just happens to be a former Blue Peter presenter.

programme.title: Inside No. 9
programme.desc: Webster, the famous barrister, is not well and not an easy man to look after. His carer Urban knows how to take the rough with the smooth, dishing out the drugs or appealing to the old man’s vanity. But on this particular night, something is coming that will test Webster’s resolve and legal skills to the very end.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Paddy O'Connell.

programme.title: Wimbledon
programme.desc: A look back at the day’s play at Wimbledon.

programme.title: T20 Cricket
programme.desc: Highlights of the fourth game of the T20 series between England and India.

programme.title: To Think Like a Killer
programme.desc: When the FBI calls, Dr Ann Burgess joins a new task force hunting serial predators and is thrust into an active investigation involving the assault of over 20 women.

programme.title: To Think Like a Killer
programme.desc: Ann delves deeper into the dark minds of serial killers while balancing her life as a mother of four. Meanwhile, the Behaviour Science Unit races to prove profiling works.

programme.title: To Think Like a Killer
programme.desc: Ann’s landmark study reshapes criminal investigation. Serial crime falls, but the battle is only just beginning.

programme.title: Brexit: A Very British Civil War
programme.desc: As David Cameron wins a shock majority at the 2015 general election, the implications immediately dawn on all concerned. There is no coalition negotiation to stop the Conservatives from fulfilling an election pledge to hold a referendum on whether Britain should leave the EU. Nigel Farage fails to win a seat and resigns as UKIP leader but quickly U-turns. He sets up Leave.EU to campaign for Brexit. Meanwhile, as Cameron continues his negotiation with the EU, his demands are watered down. It makes it even more important he recruits Boris Johnson and Michael Gove to the Remain campaign - but they haven't yet made a decision.

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: A persistent and abusive caller causes major disruption for the team in control, and crewmates Sarah and Louise are shaken after an attack on them by a patient having a seizure.

programme.title: Expert Witness
programme.desc: A forensic toxicologist proves that an Essex couple’s cause of death points to murder, and a footprint expert links a killer to two different murder scenes.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Marcus Wareing Simply Provence
programme.desc: Marcus loves a British fruit-based pudding and is searching Provence for the local equivalent. After trying out a prawn cocktail served in a half-melon on local farmers, he changes tack and meets with a local chef for a France v Britain pudding cook-off. He’s also looking for the tastiest local cheese to take to the village petanque game. He discovers a Provençal favourite is goat's cheese, and so he visits a local producer who gives him a whirlwind apprenticeship in all things goat.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Gardeners’ World comes from the very first RHS Badminton Flower Show, set in the beautiful south Gloucestershire countryside. Adam Frost, Rachel De Thame and Ashley Edwards explore the show gardens, young designer gardens, feature gardens, pocket planting and artisan gardens, and seek out the season's must-have plants in the floral marquee. There are also oodles of practical gardening advice and innovative design to inspire you in your garden.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig takes a look at animals that begin with the letter ‘T’ with Hannah Waddingham, Desiree Burch, Jason Manford, and Alan Davies.

programme.title: Newsnight

programme.title: Wimbledon

programme.title: Atonement
programme.desc: Thirteen-year-old fledgling writer Briony Tallis irrevocably changes the course of several lives when she accuses her older sister's lover of a crime he did not commit.

programme.title: Lost and Found in the Lakes
programme.desc: The scubas are out and about, determined to try to help Stephen put right a wrong when he lost his dad’s prized possession 36 years ago, while on the same lake the freedivers attempt to rescue Richard’s wedding ring, which he lost only a week before. Crafter Dave transforms a rusty old anchor beyond recognition.

programme.title: Natalie Cassidy: Caring Together
programme.desc: Natalie speaks to a friend about caring for a child with cancer before joining community nursing in action - and faces her own fear, needles!

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: When Nugget’s favourite teddy bear gets accidentally washed by Pancake, the Piripenguins try to restore its unique smell by painstakingly recreating Nugget’s happiest memories.

programme.title: Big Lizard
programme.desc: Ammon gets spooked by a tiny insect, but overcomes her fear to rescue Cosima and Big Lizard from the rapids.

programme.title: The Weasy Family
programme.desc: Weasy smashes a hole in the grain store with his boomerang. The farmer thinks his cow is to blame and decides to sell it. The ducklings can't believe Weasy would allow the cow to take the blame - they need to sort this out!

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: Mr Friendlyface’s hat has been stolen by a Big Ole Worm, and the heroes must quest to get it back. As they trail the worm through the Prickly Desert, Trevor accidentally spikes himself all over on a cactus. But all is not lost – now that he is covered in sharp spikes, Trevor realises that he has a unique superpower, and when the time comes to confront the Big Ole Worm, it’s Spiky Trevor to the rescue!

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings are throwing a big birthday party, but Grizzy is not in the mood.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck is in charge of a campsite and has to make sure everyone follows the rules. However, Frog turns up and breaks them all - he lets goats loose and has a midnight party in his tent.

programme.title: Pokémon Horizons
programme.desc: Roy and his Pokémon face Nemona in a match-up of powerful battling styles!

programme.title: Winx Club - The Magic Is Back
programme.desc: When Stella’s ring mysteriously disappears, the Winx embark on a risky mission to retrieve it from Cloud Tower. As they explore the school’s eerie tunnels, Bloom faces a powerful awakening.

programme.title: The Next Step: Cheer
programme.desc: When a legendary coach abruptly leaves a highly successful cheerleading gym, the team are left in disarray, The Next Step’s Kenzie steps in to take over what’s left of the team, desperate to prove she is more than just a dancer, and that she can build this rag-tag group of athletes into a contender for nationals.

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Shini meets one of the biggest pop stars on the planet, Harry Styles, during his record-breaking run of shows at Wembley Stadium! Plus, we go behind the scenes at Wimbledon to try all the jobs that keep the tournament on track.

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Topical news magazine for children.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Gardeners’ World comes from the very first RHS Badminton Flower Show, set in the beautiful south Gloucestershire countryside. Adam Frost, Rachel De Thame and Ashley Edwards explore the show gardens, young designer gardens, feature gardens, pocket planting and artisan gardens, and seek out the season's must-have plants in the floral marquee. There are also oodles of practical gardening advice and innovative design to inspire you in your garden.

programme.title: This Wild Life
programme.desc: Wildlife expert Saba Douglas-Hamilton, together with her husband Frank and their three young children, move to the romantic Samburu National Reserve in Kenya to run a safari camp for the first time. But life out there isn't easy. There are monkeys in the kitchen, floods threatening the camp, and with no fences to keep out the wildlife, elephants are regular house guests. This series follows their ups and downs as the family struggles to keep guests, elephants, lions and even crocodiles smiling. Things get off to a rocky start when guests arrive earlier than expected and an elephant emergency pushes everyone to their limits. Has the family got what it takes to survive their new wild life?

programme.title: Talking Tennis
programme.desc: Tennis addict Alistair McGowan serves up another affectionate look back at one of the world's greatest tournaments - Wimbledon. Here he looks at the champions who, over the years, transformed the game, setting new levels of excellence and in some cases shocking us all with their antics. With classic interviews and rarely seen footage from the BBC's archives, this episode features Billie Jean King and Arthur Ashe breaking down barriers, Becker and Borg representing youth and (male) beauty, Cash and Agassi and the game's fashion changers, Henman and Murray changing Britain's expectations of success and the most changeable feature of any Wimbledon fortnight - the weather.

programme.title: Wimbledon
programme.desc: Live coverage of the Championships at the All England Lawn Tennis Club at Wimbledon.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning is in charge of today’s Bargain Hunt, which comes from Ballinderry in Northern Ireland. The experts are Ben Cooper and Charles Hanson, who will be helping today’s teams spend £300 on three items that could make them a profit at auction. Anita also pays a visit to one of Belfast’s well-known water holes, the Crown Bar.

programme.title: North by Northwest
programme.desc: Classic Alfred Hitchcock thriller. When advertising executive Roger Thornhill is mistaken for the mysterious Mr Kaplan by a ruthless espionage ring, he soon faces deadly peril, framed for murder. On the run, Thornhill seeks Kaplan, the one person who can verify his story.

programme.title: Vertigo
programme.desc: Suddenly dangerously disorientated by heights, detective John Ferguson resigns from the San Francisco police and is reluctant when asked by an old college friend to trail the man's wife, Madeleine, whose behaviour is causing concern. But John gradually becomes obsessed with the troubled woman and increasingly intervenes in her life. Classic Hitchcock psychological thriller.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Wrest Park, a Georgian manor house surrounded by 18th-century formal gardens in the Bedfordshire countryside. Antiques experts Anita Manning and Christina Trevanion select the best antiques and collectibles to take to auction. Paul also takes a stroll down the high street of Amersham to explore the market town's unusual and varied history.

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Margie Cooper and Ishy Khan are joined by former titans of the 90s Gladiators ring, Diane ‘Jet’ Youdale and James ‘Hunter’ Crossley. They swap pugil sticks for haggling in and around the shops of Lancashire in their tiny classic Fiat 500 car, teaming up with experts Ishy Khan and Margie Cooper. In Eccleston, Diane uncovers a unique sculptural piece, while in Longridge, James bags a buy that reminds him of his beloved dog. In Darwen, there’s a sporty stop off in store as our foursome learn about a legendary local strongman, and the Gladiators show off some of their own strength. But who will be successful on the auction travellator?

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Classic wartime sitcom. Captain Mainwaring and his platoon are instructed to guard a captive U-boat crew. Are they up to the job?

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: The Top of the Pops archives are opened once again as the show moves into the 21st century. Kylie gets us spinning, whilst The Corrs leave us Breathless in a year that saw the return of legends such as Bon Jovi and U2. Dance music and hip-hop were regulars in the chart too, with hits from Destiny’s Child, Sonique and Eminem. Plus a generous sprinkling of good shiny pop. Featuring Kylie, Ronan Keating, The Corrs, Blink-182, Spiller, Baha Men, Destiny’s Child, Eminem, Bon Jovi, Sonique, Robbie Williams, Britney Spears, Craig David, All Saints, Black Legend, Joey Negro and Taka Boom, S Club and U2.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Top of the Pops officially enters the new millennium. In a year that saw the highest number of individual number ones, dance music is on the rise (again) thanks to the boom in filesharing, helping DJs such as Sonique and Spiller top the charts. Craig David takes a seemingly permanent residence in the Top 10, whilst pop continues to dominate not just the chart but TV sets too, with S Club back for their second album and second TV series. Meanwhile, American hip-hop is inbound, whilst the show still has room for more eclectic bands such as Eels. Contributors include Craig David, Sonique, S Club, Spiller, Darude, Scott Robinson from 5ive, Eels’ Mark E Everett and broadcasters Trevor Nelson, Pete Tong and Sarah Cawood.

programme.title: Radio 2 In Concert
programme.desc: Southampton-born R&B and garage king Craig David performs an intimate show in the BBC’s Radio Theatre in central London for Radio 2. He and his band perform tracks from his recent, seventh album The Time Is Now as well as classics from his best-selling debut Born To Do It, which was released in 2000 and spawned classics like Fill Me In and 7 Days.

programme.title: Wimbledon
programme.desc: A look back at the day’s play at Wimbledon.

programme.title: Golf: Scottish Open
programme.desc: Highlights from Saturday’s play as a stacked field featuring the world’s best players compete for the Genesis Scottish Open at The Renaissance Club in East Lothian. Masters Champion Rory McIlroy and World Number One Scottie Scheffler headline a stellar start list for the event which takes place a week before the 154th Open Championship. American Chris Gotterup is in great form as he looks to defend the title he won in 2025, with the home challengers including PGA Champion and 2020 Scottish Open winner Aaron Rai, 2024 winner Bob McIntyre, along with Tommy Fleetwood, Tyrrell Hatton, Marco Penge and the Fitzpatrick brothers.

But that's not freaky enough, so finally he focuses on a dinosaur that head-butted its rivals to death - something Steve's hoping to replicate with the mother of all robots. Weird!

So, Buddy makes bright green spinach pancakes stacked between layers of ham and an oozy cheese sauce - delicious and sure to knock the socks off her dancer friends! She can have it ready to go before dance class and simply pop it in the oven when she gets home. Maisie teaches Buddy to fingerspell his name in British Sign Language, and he tries to walk in Maisie’s very fast footsteps as he has a go at Irish dancing. Join Buddy, Conker and Maisie as they make a pancake stack that’s flippin’ delicious and will be sure to put a spring in your step, too!
